texto: Though the 'Thou' is not an 'It', it is also not "another 'I'". He who treats a person as "another 'I'" does not really see that person but only a projected image of himself. Such a relation, despite the warmest "personal" feeling is really 'I'-'It'.
explicacao: Citação de Mauric Friedman sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “Though the 'Thou' is not an 'It', it is also not "another 'I'". He who treats a person as "anothe…” articula autoestima, psychology, self em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como self.
